entries.text: Starting to document this properly. Building a digital growth agency from scratch — no clients yet, no playbook, just figuring out the first real steps: who I want to serve, what I can actually deliver, and how to get the first conversation.

Digital Growth Agency — Offering website, Google Business Growth, Local SEO, Social Media Growth, AI Automation, Branding and Design, Content Creation, Reputation Management, Analytics & Reporting, Maintenance, Paid Advertising, Online Commerce.

📈 DAILY PROGRESS REPORT — SAKSHAM BASNET
Date: July 7, 2026

💡 Knowledge I Gained
Learned how websites can become recurring income instead of one-time sales.
Understood the difference between selling a website and selling business growth.
Learned what services a Digital Growth Agency can eventually offer.
Learned the basics of Meta Paid Advertising and how advertising works.
Learned how to use AI as a business strategist instead of just asking it to generate websites.
Understood how agencies create recurring monthly revenue through maintenance, SEO, hosting, and other ongoing services.

🎯 Decisions I Made
I will build a Digital Growth Agency, not just a website business.
I will focus on helping local businesses increase customers and improve their online presence.
I will create service packages instead of simply quoting website prices.
I will continue learning paid advertising because it will become one of my agency's future services.

🛠️ Tasks to Complete
☐ Learn Meta Ads Manager in depth.
☐ Learn Google Ads basics.
☐ Develop a client onboarding process.

🚀 Long-Term Vision
I want to build a company that local businesses trust to grow their business online. Instead of saying "I build websites," I want people to say "If you want your business to grow online, contact Saksham."

📝 Reflection
Today wasn't about learning another technical skill — it was about defining the business I want to build. I now have a clearer direction: create a Digital Growth Agency that combines websites, digital marketing, AI, and automation to help local businesses grow while building recurring monthly revenue. This vision gives me a roadmap for what I need to learn and build over the coming months.

📈 META ADS PROGRESS

Today marked another step toward becoming a digital growth strategist. I completed a foundational study session on Meta Ads, gaining a clear understanding of how Meta Business Suite connects Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp into one advertising ecosystem.

✨ Topics covered:
• Campaign Objectives
• Ad Formats
• Budgeting Methods
• Buying Types & Bidding
• CPC Fundamentals
• Audience Targeting
• Custom Audiences
• Placements & Scheduling
• Campaign → Ad Set → Ad Structure

🎯 Outcome:
I now understand how Meta campaigns are planned before spending a single rupee. This knowledge forms the backbone for running profitable campaigns for future clients and my own digital growth agency.

💡 Key Lesson:
A successful ad campaign isn't built on a large budget—it is built on the right objective, audience, creative, and continuous optimization.

✍️ DAILY JOURNAL

Every expert in digital marketing started by learning the fundamentals, and today was one of those important foundation-building days for me.

Instead of rushing to launch advertisements, I focused on understanding how Meta's advertising system actually works—from campaign objectives and audience targeting to bidding, placements, and budgeting.

The more I study, the more I realize that paid advertising is a strategic skill rather than simply paying for visibility. Every campaign begins with understanding people, choosing the right objective, and making data-driven decisions.

Brick by brick, I'm building the knowledge that will eventually allow me to help businesses grow through effective digital marketing and paid advertising. This isn't just learning Meta Ads—it's investing in the future of my digital growth agency.

Today was focused entirely on Meta Ads. Instead of rushing through videos, I wanted to understand how everything connects from start to finish. I learned how the Meta Ads ecosystem is structured, beginning with the ad account, Meta Pixel, and the three-level hierarchy of Campaign → Ad Set → Ad.

I also explored the complete campaign creation workflow, including budget settings, audience customization, placements, publishing ads, and monitoring performance after launch. Rather than just memorizing steps, I tried to understand why each setting exists and how changing one decision can affect the final result.

Another major part of today's learning was understanding the metrics that actually matter. I studied CPM, CTR, CPC, ROAS, and Frequency, along with concepts like Retargeting and Dynamic Ads. I also learned how the Meta Ads Library can be used to study competitors and analyze active advertisements.

Instead of keeping everything in my head, I documented every important point in my notebook. These notes will make revision easier and help me build a stronger understanding before I start running real campaigns.

There's still a lot more to learn, but today's session gave me a much clearer picture of how Meta Ads work from setup to optimization.

Meta Ads — Learning Notes

Campaign Structure
• Ad Account
• Meta Pixel
• Campaign → Ad Set → Ad

Campaign Creation
• Create campaign
• Choose objective
• Budget setup
• Audience customization
• Placements
• Publish campaign
• Monitor performance

Important Metrics
• CPM
• CTR
• CPC
• ROAS
• Frequency

Advanced Topics
• Retargeting
• Dynamic Ads
• Meta Ads Library
• Learning Phase
• Scaling campaigns

Key Takeaways
• Video ads generally perform better.
• ROAS is one of the most important metrics when deciding whether to scale a campaign.
• The Ads Library is useful for competitor research and creative inspiration.
• Every campaign should be monitored after publishing instead of being left alone.

📈 DAILY PROGRESS REPORT — SAKSHAM BASNET

📅 Date: July 9, 2026

💡 What I Learned

• Understood the complete Meta Ads ecosystem.
• Learned how Meta Pixel connects with advertising campaigns.
• Studied the Campaign → Ad Set → Ad hierarchy.
• Learned how campaigns are created from start to finish.
• Explored budget management, audience targeting, placements, and campaign publishing.
• Learned how to monitor campaign performance after publishing.
• Studied the most important advertising metrics: CPM, CTR, CPC, ROAS, and Frequency.
• Learned about Retargeting and Dynamic Ads.
• Explored the Meta Ads Library for competitor research.
• Understood why video ads usually outperform static creatives.

✅ Progress Made

☑ Completed detailed handwritten notes.
☑ Built a stronger understanding of Meta Ads fundamentals.
☑ Connected multiple concepts instead of learning them individually.
☑ Improved my understanding of how campaigns are structured and optimized.

🎯 Next Focus

☐ Practice inside Meta Ads Manager.
☐ Learn campaign objectives in detail.
☐ Build a sample campaign.
☐ Continue learning optimization and scaling strategies.

📝 Reflection

Today's goal wasn't to memorize features—it was to understand how the whole system fits together. By writing detailed notes and breaking the process into smaller parts, I now have a much clearer understanding of how Meta Ads are structured, launched, monitored, and improved. I still have plenty to learn, but today's work gave me a solid foundation to build on before running real advertising campaigns.

Today I worked on building a Digital Business Social Card. The idea is simple: instead of sharing multiple social media usernames or links, a customer only needs to tap an NFC card or scan a QR code to access everything in one place.

I spent my time designing and improving the web page that acts as the digital profile. The goal was to make it clean, mobile-friendly, and easy to navigate so businesses can share all of their important links through a single landing page.

While building it, I also started thinking beyond the website itself. This isn't just another web project—it could become a service that local businesses actually use. Restaurants, cafés, freelancers, agencies, and small businesses could replace printed business cards with a smart digital card that is faster to share and easier to update.

The project is still in development, but today's work helped me understand how NFC technology, QR codes, and web development can be combined into a practical product instead of just another website.

Digital Business Social Card

Project Overview
• A smart digital profile that allows customers to access all business information through a single interaction.

Core Features
• NFC Tap Support
• QR Code Access
• Centralized Social Links
• Contact Information
• Business Location
• Website Links
• Mobile Responsive Design
• Easy to Update

Possible Use Cases
• Cafés
• Restaurants
• Salons
• Gyms
• Freelancers
• Agencies
• Local Businesses

Main Value
• Customers only need to tap an NFC card or scan a QR code to instantly access every important business link from one place.

📈 DAILY PROGRESS REPORT — SAKSHAM BASNET

📅 Date: July 10, 2026

🚀 Project Worked On

Digital Business Social Card (NFC + QR)

💡 What I Built

• Developed the landing page for a Digital Business Social Card.
• Integrated the concept of NFC tap and QR scan into a single web experience.
• Organized business information into one clean, mobile-friendly page.
• Designed the project to work as a reusable solution for different businesses.

🧠 What I Learned

• Better understanding of how NFC and QR technologies can work together.
• Learned how digital business cards improve accessibility compared to traditional printed cards.
• Improved my frontend development and UI organization.
• Started thinking about the product as a business solution rather than only a web page.

🎯 Ideas From Today's Work

☑ Businesses can share all their social links with one tap.
☑ Information can be updated without printing new business cards.
☑ The same system can be customized for different industries.
☑ This could become one of the products offered through my Digital Growth Agency.

🛠️ Next Steps

☐ Improve UI and animations.
☐ Add more customization options.
☐ Test NFC cards with physical tags.
☐ Prepare a demo for local businesses.

📝 Reflection

Today's work wasn't just about building another website. It was about turning a simple idea into something businesses could actually use. The project combines web development, NFC technology, and QR codes into one practical solution. There are still improvements to make, but the foundation is now in place.

Today I switched between two projects instead of focusing on one. First, I reworked DhanSetu, where I redesigned the UI and kept a backup of the current version. I didn't make major functional changes, but having a backup gives me the confidence to continue experimenting without worrying about losing previous work.

The second part of the day was spent thinking about building a clipping agency. I found a friend who's willing to work with me. The initial plan is simple: I'll handle finding campaigns and managing the workflow while he'll be responsible for editing, posting, and pushing content. If the model works, I already have a blueprint in mind to expand it into a complete agency under my company, SAKX.

Nothing has officially started yet, but I spent a lot of time planning how everything could operate. In my head, I already see myself running the company. Now the only thing left is to build it step by step and make that vision real.

Projects Worked On

DhanSetu
• Redesigned the UI.
• Created a backup of the current version.
• Prepared a safer foundation for future development.

Clipping Agency
• Planned the initial workflow.
• Team Structure:
  - Me → Campaign sourcing, management, operations.
  - Friend → Editing, posting, content execution.

Long-Term Vision
• Validate the clipping model first.
• Expand it into a complete agency.
• Operate it under SAKX.
• Build systems before scaling the team.

📈 DAILY PROGRESS REPORT — SAKSHAM BASNET

📅 Date: July 11, 2026

🚀 Projects Worked On

1. DhanSetu

☑ Reworked the UI.
☑ Created a backup before making further changes.
☑ Improved the project's readiness for future development.

2. Clipping Agency

☑ Defined the initial operating model.
☑ Brought my first team member onboard.
☑ Split responsibilities between management and execution.

💡 Progress Made

• Continued improving DhanSetu instead of leaving it unfinished.
• Took the first practical step toward building a clipping agency.
• Started thinking beyond freelancing by designing a business structure instead of just completing tasks.
• Created an initial blueprint for expanding the clipping service into a full agency under SAKX.

🎯 Next Steps

☐ Find and test the first clipping campaign.
☐ Build a workflow for campaign tracking and content delivery.
☐ Continue refining DhanSetu's UI and features.
☐ Turn the agency blueprint into an actionable roadmap.

📝 Reflection

Today was less about coding and more about building systems. DhanSetu moved forward with a cleaner UI and a proper backup, while the clipping agency moved from just an idea to an actual plan with defined roles. The company doesn't exist yet, but the foundation is starting to take shape. Every decision I make now is focused on building something that can grow under SAKX, not just completing individual projects.

Today was dedicated to YUVAX. Most of the work went into refining the website before making it public. I redesigned the WhatsApp banner multiple times, adjusted the layout, improved the image quality, and made sure the branding looked clean and consistent across the page. It took several iterations to get the banner aligned properly without overlapping elements or losing important content.

After finishing the final touches, I deployed the website and officially launched it online. Seeing the project move from development to a live website felt like an important milestone. It's no longer just another project sitting on my computer—it's now something people can actually visit and use.

I also worked on setting up the business email and explored the initial steps required for integrating the WhatsApp Business API, giving me a better understanding of how the communication side of the agency will work in the future.

YUVAX - Website Launch

Completed
• Finalized website UI.
• Redesigned and optimized the WhatsApp banner.
• Improved banner quality and alignment.
• Deployed the website to production.
• Created the business email.
• Explored Meta WhatsApp Business API setup.

Key Learnings
• Small UI adjustments have a big impact on presentation.
• Branding consistency matters before launching.
• Launching a project is only the beginning; maintaining and improving it is an ongoing process.

📈 DAILY PROGRESS REPORT — SAKSHAM BASNET

📅 Date: July 12, 2026

🚀 Project

YUVAX — Digital Growth Agency

✅ Work Completed

☑ Finalized the YUVAX website.

☐ Complete WhatsApp Business integration.
☐ Continue refining the website.
☐ Prepare service pages and portfolio updates.
☐ Start reaching out to potential clients.

📝 Reflection

Today's biggest achievement wasn't writing code—it was shipping a real project. After several rounds of improvements and refinements, YUVAX is finally live. Launching it marks the beginning of building an agency with a public presence. From here, the focus shifts from building the foundation to improving it and bringing in the first clients.

Today I went back to the NFC + QR Digital Business Card project and focused on improving its control panel. The plan was to add more features so businesses could manage their digital profile more efficiently without modifying the website manually.

I experimented with different ideas and tried implementing new functionality, but none of the approaches worked the way I expected. After spending time debugging and testing, I decided not to force incomplete features into the project. Instead, I left it in a stable state and noted the ideas for later.

Although I didn't ship any new features today, the session wasn't wasted. It helped me understand the current limitations of the control panel and highlighted areas that need a better structure before more functionality can be added. Sometimes knowing what doesn't work is just as useful as building something that does.

NFC + QR Digital Business Card

Focus
• Worked on expanding the control panel.

Attempted
• Planned additional management features.
• Tested different implementation approaches.
• Debugged issues during development.

Findings
• Current control panel needs a better structure before adding more features.
• Some planned features require a different implementation approach.
• Keeping the project stable is better than adding unfinished functionality.

📈 DAILY PROGRESS REPORT — SAKSHAM BASNET

📅 Date: July 13, 2026

🚀 Project

NFC + QR Digital Business Card

💻 Work Completed

☑ Revisited the project after previous development.
☑ Worked on expanding the control panel.
☑ Tested multiple feature ideas.
☑ Investigated implementation issues.

💡 What I Learned

• The current architecture isn't ready for some of the planned features.
• Building management tools requires better planning than simply adding new UI elements.
• Debugging exposed weaknesses that should be addressed before continuing development.

🎯 Next Steps

☐ Redesign the control panel structure.
☐ Break new features into smaller modules.
☐ Fix the underlying issues before adding more functionality.
☐ Resume feature development once the foundation is stable.

📝 Reflection

Not every development session ends with a new feature. Today's progress came from testing ideas, identifying limitations, and understanding where the project needs improvement. Rather than forcing features into an unstable system, I chose to stop, evaluate the current structure, and plan a better approach for the next iteration.
